title: Plumb through the --stop-timeout signal handling
body:
podman run/create have the ability to set the stop timeout flag.
We need to stop it in the database.

Also Allowing negative time for stop timeout makes no sense, so switching
to timeout of uint, allows user to specify huge timeout values.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I think you should make the timeout parameter to container.Stop() a pointer, which gives us a way of knowing whether we should use it. Then we can make `podman stop` only use it if the timeout flag is set there explicitly.

Should also make runtime.RemoveContainer use the container's StopTimeout instead of its constant, I think?
@rhatdan I still don't like it, but I don't see a better way forward. LGTM. If we figure out a better way of handling it in the future, we can add that.

:pushpin: Commit b298edb has been approved by `TomSweeneyRedHat`
